candidates.notes: H7 FAILED: Whitened RepSim DEGRADED performance on all 3 tasks (-8 to -11pp). Root cause analysis: (1) N=100 << d=2048 makes covariance estimation unreliable despite Ledoit-Wolf shrinkage; (2) Heavy shrinkage (0.12-0.40) pulls Sigma toward scaled identity, which should approximate standard cosine -- but the inversion amplifies estimation error in low-variance directions; (3) The per-query SNR analysis shows weak but positive correlation with accuracy (r=0.34 on counterfact), suggesting the concept has merit but the estimator is too noisy at pilot scale. RECOMMENDATION: H7 requires N >> d (at least N=5000+) for meaningful whitening. At pilot scale, this is an expected failure mode. The negative result is informative -- it shows that naive whitening hurts, supporting the theoretical prediction that Sigma_noise estimation quality is critical. For the paper, this positions whitening as a promising theoretical direction that requires careful regularization, not an out-of-the-box improvement.

h7_detailed_analysis.primary_result: FAIL -- whitened < standard on all 3 tasks
root_causes: N/d ratio = 100/2048 = 0.049 -- severely underdetermined covariance estimation
Ledoit-Wolf shrinkage coefficients (0.12-0.40) indicate heavy regularization needed
Covariance inverse amplifies estimation noise in low-variance directions
Whitened scores have very high absolute values (~2000-3500) with small relative spread, making ranking fragile
positive_signals: Ledoit-Wolf converges successfully on all tasks
Per-query SNR shows weak positive correlation with accuracy (concept is directionally sound)
Whitening does change rankings substantially (rank deltas of 50-87 positions)
recommendations_for_full_experiment: Use N >= 5000 training samples for stable covariance estimation
Try PCA-reduced whitening: project to top-k PCA dimensions first, then whiten in lower-dim space
Implement shrinkage-intensity sweep to find optimal regularization
Consider oracle Sigma (from held-out data) to establish upper bound on whitening benefit
Report H7 as 'promising theoretical direction, requires careful regularization' rather than claiming improvement
